Transcrição Completa: 6 Implementing AI Setters - Zac Taverner

[00:20] "So, uh by the end of this presentation, you'll know how to implement an AI setter into your business or your clients to skyrocket appointments and sales."

[01:22] "So just to be clear that means 15 people are on the field 80 minute run time and then sevens is 14 minutes long and only seven people on one side of the team 14 in total."

NÚMEROS: #1 team in nation, 70 team members, 2 championships, 80 minutes (15s), 14 minutes (7s)
VISUAL: Layout dividido em duas colunas com ícones de velocidade e execução, troféus de campeonato, comparação visual entre formatos 15s e 7s

[02:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "AI Market Impact - Forbes & Goldman Sachs Data"

[02:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Critical Sales Statistics"
NÚMEROS: 92% don't follow up after 4th touch, 80% of sales happen between 5th-12th touch, 78% buy from first responder
VISUAL: Três gráficos de barras mostrando percentuais em cores contrastantes (vermelho para negativo, verde para positivo), ícones de follow-up e velocidade de resposta

[02:58] "Okay. So, can your business handle an additional thousand leads in the next 30 minutes and ensure they're followed up five plus times? Any of you guys?"
[03:08] "No, woo sender doesn't work."
[03:11] "Uh, are you using AI right now?"
[03:13] "Yeah. How's it going? Good."
[03:16] "Yeah. Cool. Is there anybody else using AI right now for appointments or sales? You. Cool. Awesome."
[03:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Two Ways to Handle Leads"
NÚMEROS: 1 vs 2 (Humans vs AI Setters)
VISUAL: Layout comparativo dividido ao meio, lado esquerdo com ícone humano, lado direito com ícone de robô/IA, versus no centro
[03:28] "So, there's two ways to handle the leads. Number one, humans. And two, AI setters. There's a little graphic for you guys."
[03:37] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Humans - Limitations"
NÚMEROS: 1-8 conversations simultaneously, 3 minutes to 3 hours response time
VISUAL: Lista com ícones vermelhos/negativos: relógio para speed to lead, gráfico inconsistente, cifrão alto, gráfico descendente de conversão, erros de texto, pessoa com múltiplas conversas limitadas
[03:40] "So, let's take a look at humans. So, speed to lead notoriously, depending on how busy they are, whether it's night or day, it's going to take some time for them to get um anywhere to actually reach out to the lead or call them depending on what time it is."
[03:55] "And then inconsistent response time. So, it might be 3 minutes or it might be three hours just depending on the business and the size of them."
[04:03] "high overhead costs, um poor conversion rate, spelling and grammar errors, and then managing one to eight conversations at a time."
[04:13] "So, as humans, we're limited to the amount of conversations that we can actually have across different platforms. And the AI setter solves that."
[04:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "AI Setters - Advantages"
NÚMEROS: 1-2 minute response time, unlimited conversations, fraction of a penny cost, 5 messages before detection
VISUAL: Lista com ícones verdes/positivos: relógio rápido, piloto automático, FAQ personalizado, cifrão baixo, checkmark para gramática, símbolo infinito para conversas ilimitadas
[04:25] "So, let's go ahead take a look at them. So, it's a 1 to two minute response time. Now, you can actually edit the amount of time that you want between those response times."
[04:35] "So to make it look more human, you can put a delay in of 1 to 2 minutes. So that way it's not instantaneous response and by message number five, they're like, 'Is this an AI or is it a bot?'"
[04:47] "Um, you can kind of change that to make it look more humanistic, right?"
[04:52] "Um, sales and appointments on autopilot custom FAQ. So, as you deploy this into your business and your clients, you can adjust the FAQ to where, let's say, for example, somebody asked a question that you previously did not have in the FAQ."
[05:08] "You can go in update it with the way that you want your business to respond to that question. And then now the next conversations of somebody who asked that question, maybe they asked it in a different way, the AI will actually be able to respond instantaneously to that response the correct way."
[05:24] "Sometimes it does lie and make up stuff. So you want to make sure those FAQs are uh are put in place correctly."
[05:31] "Um low operational costs. So this is like fraction of a penny to operate. Um when you compare that with setters you know that's going to be very expensive comparison uh but it's on a per message basis."
[05:44] "So new message comes in AI puts in an output and then it responds on the channel that uh the customer responded to."
[05:53] "So errorfree grammar and spelling this is good and bad. It's uh great because your clients aren't worried about somebody overseas spelling something wrong."
[06:04] "Um, and then on top of that, you can make the AI have some errors and emojis in it just for a more human conversational aspect of it."
[06:13] "Um, but that way you don't look bad when uh your clients see your messages back and forth."
[06:19] "And then it can handle unlimited amount of conversations. So whether you get a,000 leads or 10,000 leads in the next hour, it can handle every single one of them within the first five minutes."
[06:31] "and then follow up with them throughout the first day, first hour, four hours later, one week, etc., whatever your follow-up cadence is, and then respond when that person is ready to actually have the conversation, right?"
[06:44] "So, sometimes, you know, it's 1:00 a.m., 2 a.m., they're back at their phone and on Instagram DMs. You want to respond to them when they're ready to have that conversation."
[06:53] "And remember, 78% of customers purchase from the business that reach out the first. Right?"

[07:15] "Um, the setters and the sales reps were lazy and they weren't getting to the leads fast enough. And so, we implemented this and saw a 44% increase in booked appointments."

[08:30] "And then the AI responded within that first minute. Right now, it's a little bit long, but it's great to hear. Think you grow rich movie features exclusive footage from Bob Procttor, right?"
[08:42] "It actually took what she said in the previous message and put it in the first side of it, as well as other successful individuals like Grant Cardone, Barbara um Lewis House."
[08:53] "The movie uncovers 13 timeless secrets of Think You Grow Rich movie. You can purchase the movie for $37 and then it goes into would you like the purchase link?"
[09:03] "She got the purchase link and then purchased the movie right after that. And I guarantee this person would not have purchased if we didn't get back that fast to her and sent her the link again to get her to purchase. Right."

[09:34] "So then that way it's just not some long block of text. Sometimes it does, but um that one specifically we want to make it more conversational based than like it's an AI bot, right?"

[10:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Conversation Flow Example - Trading Bot"
NÚMEROS: 3-4 qualifying questions, passive income goal mentioned

[10:20] "Okay. Do you have any experience in trading or prop firms? And then it said it's new to me. Got it. This is the AI."
[10:28] "What's the main goal you are looking to accomplish with trading? They said, I want to make passive income on the side."
[10:35] "AI says, understood. What would you say is holding you back from achieving your goal of making passive income through trading? So, it actually pulls out their desire and puts it into the next message."
[10:48] "And then, I'm not that experienced. Okay, got it. if you're open to a quick demo, I can show you how our AI trading, you know, goes into the thing. How would you like that?"
[10:59] "And then it goes through and actually books that appointment and that person became a sale, right? So, it's the AI reaching out to the leads, conversing with them, and then ultimately booking that appointment right afterwards."
[11:12] "And this was actually from text to an application. So, it sent a link, they filled out the application, booked the appointment, and then went to the next step."

[11:56] "U 53 booked appointments from there. So that's a 23% conversion rate from app to call and then eight sales. So 15% closing rate at 3500 is 28K in additional revenue with AI."

[13:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "AI Setter Overview - 5 Workflows"
NÚMEROS: 5 distinct workflows
VISUAL: Diagrama de fluxo com 5 caixas conectadas: 1) Activation 2) Response from Prospects 3) AI Response 4) Re-engagement 5) Stop AI, setas conectando cada etapa, cores diferentes para cada workflow
[13:26] "U so AI setter overview. So, we got five different uh workflows here. So, essentially, you got your activation. So, we're going to activate the AI."
[13:37] "Um, we're going to get responses from prospects. Um, the AI is going to respond and then we have a re-engagement for people who don't respond right away."
[13:47] "We want to follow up with them, re-engage them, and then get them to convert whether it's into appointments or sales and then ultimately stop the AI."
[13:56] "So, when a booked appointment happens, it roundrobins to the next sales rep and uh they'll be able to take over the conversation from there."
[14:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Workflow #1: Activation"
NÚMEROS: Step-by-step process flow
VISUAL: Fluxograma detalhado mostrando: new lead > add tag > check email > conditional split (tem email/não tem email) > create fake email if needed > tag "email incorrect" > activate bot, caixas conectadas com setas lógicas
[14:08] "So, here is the workflow for the first one. If you guys want to take a picture again, I'll send you the slides afterwards."
[14:14] "Uh, but essentially, new lead comes in, adds a tag. Um, this checks for an email. So, when you guys are having Instagram DMs, Facebook Messenger set up, um, Zapier actually has to track that contact."
[14:29] "And the only way we can do that is actually through having an email attached to it. Um, and so what we do is if they don't have an email attached when they come in on Instagram, we create a fake one for them, tag it, um, and then we tag that the email is incorrect."
[14:46] "So then that way it can actually converse on DMs. Then at the end of the Instagram DM, we get their phone number before booking the link, sending the link, etc."
[14:57] "Um, so then that way that contact is still tied to the booked appointments, to the application, etc. Okay, so that's the activation of the bot."
[15:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Workflow #2: Any Replies"
NÚMEROS: Conditional checks for bot status
VISUAL: Fluxograma mostrando: incoming message > check tags (Instagram DM + bot active, SMS + bot active) > conditional filter > save lead response > empty AI response field > send to Zapier, destaque para verificações condicionais
[15:11] "Um, any replies. So now you guys can't see this at the very top, but um, this is like Instagram DM plus bot, SMS plus bot is active."
[15:21] "So we want to make sure that we're only conversing with leads that h are activated with the AI, right? So, if somebody comes in and let's say it's a friend or somebody who already previously closed, we want to make sure that the bot is not interacting with them to try to close them on anything."
[15:38] "So, that's what this is for right here to make sure that doesn't happen. Um, and then right here on the on the bottom left hand side, so this saves the lead response and then empties the AI set a response and then it sends over to Zapier the prompt the response and then um, yeah, and then we'll get the AI set a response in the next workflow."
[16:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Workflow #3: AI Response"
NÚMEROS: 1 minute wait time
VISUAL: Fluxograma detalhando: Zapier return > conditional check (bot active?) > wait 1 minute > channel detection > split into SMS/Instagram/Facebook/Email paths, destaque para delay humanizado
[16:06] "So right here, the AI response. So over on Zapier, I'll show you how to set that up here in a second. Uh but it's going to come back through. It's going to have a conditional um if then statement."
[16:18] "So if the bot is active, continue. If it's not active, don't continue. And then um and you can add more to this. So like if you want uh tags of like previous buyers and things like that to not go into the AI ever, you can just add that at the top."
[16:34] "Um, and then it's going to wait one minute. So that waiting period is so it's not like instantaneous. They don't think it's a bot at up at 2 am responding to them right in one second."
[16:46] "Um, so that's what the wait period is. And as you can see at the very bottom, it checks for the channel and then it goes into whichever channel it should be in. So that's um, SMS, Instagram, Facebook DMs as well as email."
[17:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Workflow #4: Re-engagement"
NÚMEROS: Q1, Q2, Q3 question sequence, 4 hours follow-up delay
VISUAL: Fluxograma complexo mostrando múltiplos paths de perguntas qualificadoras, loops de follow-up baseados em tempo, tags para cada estágio de pergunta, setas de progressão entre Q1>Q2>Q3
[17:05] "So this is the re-engagement workflow. So typically with high ticket buyers, they ask a series of questions. Um after those questions are asked, they then set the appointment."
[17:17] "So as you can see, well you probably can't see, but at the top it has a Q1, Q3 or Q2, Q3, etc. And that's just going through the series of questions before they get to the booking link or ask for the booking appointment."
[17:32] "And um what this does is it follows up on each question. So we we customize this to where you know if you ask what industry they're in, then it will follow up and be like, 'Hey, just wanted to check back in um and curious of what industry you're in four hours later.'"
[17:50] "Then the bot will still take over, go to the next question, and then it will um take that lead out of the first Q1, then send them into the second workflow and continue that process."
[18:04] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Workflow #5: Stop AI"
NÚMEROS: Trigger points for stopping bot
VISUAL: Fluxograma mostrando: appointment booked (in CRM or Calendly) > webhook trigger > stop AI via custom field > remove bot tags > handoff to sales rep, ícones de calendário e pessoa assumindo conversa
[18:07] "All righty. And then this is the stop workflow. So, anytime a booked appointment happens in the CRM or outside the CRM if you're using Calendarly, then you pull it back in um stop the AI with a custom field and then purchases booking bot."
[18:26] "So, we've used a few different softwares um don't necessarily want to name them but who claim that they have like a booking bot and things like that that work very well."
[18:36] "We tried to implement it for one of our clients who's getting about a thousand leads a day. We it failed from the conversation all the way to the booked appointments and made us look terrible."
[18:48] "So we found this the way that we set this up is actually outperforming like those types of softwares that have an API connection because they don't break as much."
[18:59] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "GoHighLevel Booking Bot Integration"
NÚMEROS: Timeout mechanism included
VISUAL: Fluxograma mostrando integração com GHL booking bot, timeout detection, reativação de AI após timeout, loop de rebooking via texto, conexão entre sistemas
[19:02] "So from here um it goes to the gohigh level booking bot and then once it times out it asks the question and reactivates the bot."
[19:11] "So then that way it can go back through any questions that they might have and then rebook them through the booking bot. So it's all via text."
[19:20] "When you're getting where are you getting these sourcing these leads from? Is it because I see you're communicating with Facebook Messenger few other DMs and stuff like that. So where are the leads coming from?"
[19:32] "Yeah. So, typically it's going to be through ads. Um, or if they have a strong organic following, then it's like, oh, text me the word scale and then the bot can take over from there. Um get their information and then convert them into whether it's an appointment or a sale."
[19:49] "And if you're running ads and getting their contact information, are you able to contact them with the AI through DMs or is that"
[19:56] "No, no, that's going to be through text then text and email. Yeah."
[20:00] "Yeah. It's only going to be for like inbound messages for IGDMs, Facebook Messenger. So that's that's the only way to do it."
[20:08] "Yeah. So if you if you like retask your setters to do outbound instead of inbound, like they can do outbound, focus on outbound. Anything inbound, the AI setter can take over."
[20:21] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Custom Fields Setup"

[20:35] "And then uh AI set a response, AI setter reply channel. So all of those ones are used inside the zap uh which we'll go over right now."
[20:46] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How Zapier Integration Works"
NÚMEROS: 3-step process
VISUAL: Diagrama de 3 etapas conectadas: 1) Webhook catch from GHL 2) ChatGPT conversation processing 3) Update contact in GHL, setas bidirecionais, logos Zapier/GHL/ChatGPT
[20:49] "And then um so how it works is on that other workflow that I showed you where it sends the web hook into Zap um which is going to be on the response. So customer responds that sends over to Zapier."
[21:03] "it catches the web hook and then it goes through chat GBT. So conversation and chat GBT uh you want to use this specific um zap because it will remember the entire conversation and be able to follow up with them."
[21:17] "If you use like OpenAI or a couple other ones, it won't remember the conversation. Um so you want to make sure it's chat GBT and then this is just go high level. So you just add update the contact on the other side."
[21:31] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "ChatGPT Conversation Setup in Zapier"
NÚMEROS: 5 key fields to configure
VISUAL: Screenshot do Zapier mostrando campos de configuração: User Message, Username, Assistant Name, Assistant Instructions, Model (GPT-3.5), Memory Key, cada campo com exemplo preenchido e explicação
[21:35] "So the conversation in chat GBT, this is actually how you set it up on the inside. So user message is going to be the contacts message u that gets pulled over from go high level."
[21:47] "Uh username is just going to be the contact name that will be pulled out from the web hook. Um assistant name, so that would be like Ashley, whatever you want to call your your bot, Charlie, etc."
[22:00] "Um, the assistant instructions is going to be your prompt, which we'll go over in a second, but that'll include like the instructions about the AI, and then your FAQ as well, and then your model."
[22:13] "So, right now, uh, ChatGpt 3.5 is what we're using just because it's it's faster, it's quicker."
[22:21] "um once we get GPT4 out which just got released but it's not um it's not like you can you have a limit to the amount of APIs that you can call from it so it's not going to be it's not going to work for high volume at the moment"
[22:35] "but here in the next three six months it'll uh outperform 3.5 and be able to you know we'll just improve it from there essentially"
[22:44] "and then the memory key so the contact ID that gets pulled out from go high level um is going to be how it remembers who that contact is."
[22:54] "And you can do that memory key based off of their phone number, based off of their email. Um if you're using like another platform like um HubSpot, Alaware, those types of platforms."
[23:06] "Yeah, Nick. user is the is user one of the staff members on this team"
[23:11] "not how they have it set up user is more of the prospect in this case I don't know why they have it that way but"
[23:17] "it's just a different syntax"
[23:19] "correct yeah this isn't go high level this is chatbt so they just named it differently"

[24:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Prompt Example - Trading Bot"
NÚMEROS: 5 sequential questions
VISUAL: Documento de prompt mostrando: Goal statement no topo, 5 perguntas numeradas (1-5), FAQ section no final, formatação clara com bullets e números
[25:01] "Um, I don't want to read all of it, but essentially your goal is to interact with potential clients, understand their trading aspirations, abide by the key rules, and then sequentially ask each question with the aim of scheduling a consultation."
[25:18] "Okay, these are the five questions. One, two, three, four, five. So, those are the five questions that we ask before actually getting them to a um application."
[25:29] "And then the FAQ is at the very bottom. So every single business will have their own set of FAQ which will just be like, you know, what's your pricing, here's the answer, um all those generic questions that get asked all the time um that need a response."
[25:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Core Rules Section"
NÚMEROS: 340 characters max, 5th grade reading level, multiple behavioral rules
VISUAL: Lista detalhada de regras com bullets: ask one question at a time, booking URL location, character limit, assume yes strategy, personalization requirements, disqualification criteria, formatação em lista clara
[25:48] "And the second part of that is going to be core rules. So this is what the AI needs to follow um every single time."
[25:55] "So ask each question one at a time before setting the call. Here's the booking URL. So this was um because we had an application they went here instead."
[26:06] "Uh response must be under 340 characters. Assume yes as the first answer. Um explain how you can assist them and then guide them towards booking a call."
[26:17] "Use fifth grade level conversation. Uh personalize your responses based off the information they provide. Um if a prospect asks a question, answer it and then proceed with the qualifying questions."
[26:30] "Um and then these just go through more directions of what we're telling the AI to do. Um, and then it says, 'Do not book calls with prospects who don't have money or funds to invest.'"
[26:42] "So, it can dynamically change what it's saying to the prospect based off of what they give you. So, for example, if you wanted to disqualify a lead and send them to a low ticket thing instead of getting them on a um call,"
[26:57] "you can actually do that inside of the core rules by saying, you know, if they say one of these things, disqualify them. Um, and you can also pull that out from like a lead form or anything else like that."
[27:09] "What are some examples of things that they can say that you would tell"
[27:13] "if they say they have no money?"
[27:15] "Um, yeah. So, that would be like um like if they're in a certain state that you don't um service,"
[27:23] "then that would be a disqualification. If um like"
[27:27] "I guess the question the reason I'm ask is that like from my when I was doing calls and I had sales calls there are certain cues that like if we mention the price or something and the prospect kind of like winces or like makes the space like it's probably like that they can't afford it's hard to come up with that nuance on AI. So I'm just wondering"
[27:46] "Yeah. So your case would probably be employee size or uh something something along those lines to where you can like get their financials but also get the response from them."
[28:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "TCPA Compliance - SMS Timing"
NÚMEROS: 8:00 AM - 9:00 PM sending window, contact timezone based
VISUAL: Relógio mostrando janela de horário permitido (8am-9pm), ícone de fusos horários, workflow separado para SMS vs outras plataformas, destaque vermelho para restrições legais
[28:03] "All righty. And then TCPA on spam or SMS. Uh so this is when you set up the workflow. Um like this is starting to get a really big thing on SMS."
[28:14] "So you want to make sure you're only messaging between 8 and 900 p.m. every single day. U along with the AI. So, you don't want the AI messaging somebody at, you know, 2 a.m. going back and forth with them."
[28:27] "Um, and so how we set this up is we actually separate the SMS from the other platforms, put it into its own workflow, turn this on because it's there's actually a setting at the top. It's it's based off of the contact time zone."
[28:42] "Um, and so that way you're making sure that you're not messaging them outside of those hours. But on Instagram, Facebook Messenger, all of that, it's free game, so you can just message them 247."
[28:55] "Okay. Um, question and answers."
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
92% dos vendedores não fazem follow-up após o 4º toque
80% das vendas acontecem entre o 5º e 12º toque
78% dos clientes compram de quem responde primeiro
300 milhões de empregos serão impactados por IA (próximos 5 anos)
IBM planeja substituir 800.000 empregos com IA
Economia estimada de $400 milhões anuais (IBM)
CASE STUDY #1 - HIGH TICKET COACHING:
44.9% mais agendamentos
85 calls adicionais em 30 dias
CASE STUDY #2 - THINK AND GROW RICH:
4-8 vendas low ticket diárias
Ticket: $37
Tracking: UTM ?eel=AI via Hyros
CASE STUDY #3 - AI TRADING BOT (BLUE EDGE FINANCIAL):
Período: 3 semanas
2,067 leads totais
415 respostas (20% taxa de resposta)
222 aplicações (53% conversão)
53 agendamentos (23% app-to-call)
8 vendas (15% taxa de fechamento)
Ticket: $3,500
Revenue adicional: $28,000
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
VANTAGENS AI vs HUMANOS:
Tempo de resposta: 1-2 minutos (AI) vs 3 minutos-3 horas (humanos)
Conversas simultâneas: Ilimitadas (AI) vs 1-8 (humanos)
Custo operacional: Fração de centavo (AI) vs alto (humanos)
Disponibilidade: 24/7 em múltiplos canais
Gramática: Zero erros (configurável para parecer humano)
5 WORKFLOWS PRINCIPAIS:
Activation: New lead > Check email > Create fake if needed > Tag > Activate bot
Any Replies: Filter active bots > Save response > Send to Zapier
AI Response: Return from Zapier > Wait 1 min > Detect channel > Send message
Re-engagement: Q1/Q2/Q3 sequence > 4-hour follow-ups > Progress through questions
Stop AI: Appointment booked > Stop bot > Handoff to sales rep
INTEGRAÇÃO TÉCNICA:
OpenAI API (platform.openai.com/signup)
ChatGPT 3.5 (atual) / GPT-4 (futuro)
Zapier para conectar GHL + ChatGPT
GoHighLevel para CRM e booking
Canais: SMS, Instagram DM, Facebook Messenger, Email
CUSTOM FIELDS:
Contact Response
AI Setter Status (On/Off)
AI Setter Response
AI Setter Reply Channel
ESTRUTURA DE PROMPTS:
Goal statement
5 perguntas qualificadoras sequenciais
FAQ customizado por negócio
Core Rules (340 caracteres max, 5th grade level, disqualification criteria)
COMPLIANCE TCPA:
SMS: 8:00 AM - 9:00 PM (timezone do contato)
Instagram/Facebook: 24/7 sem restrições
Workflow separado para SMS
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Custo operacional: Fração de centavo por mensagem
Case #3: $28K revenue adicional em 3 semanas
ROI: Substitui múltiplos setters ($50K+ salário anual cada)
Case #2: Vendas diárias recorrentes ($37 × 4-8 = $148-296/dia)
MELHORIAS FUTURAS:
GPT-4: Maior capacidade de API (3-6 meses)
Melhor handling de conversas complexas
Aprendizado entre sub-contas (limitado atualmente)
DURAÇÃO: ~29 minutos FORMATO: Apresentação técnica/tutorial com cases práticos
